concerns.text: SC-001 requires verifying a non-monotonic 'inverted-U' trend. The tasks (T018-T022) only implement logic for two models (TinyLlama, Phi-2) and explicitly mark the 'Collapse Regime' as 'Inconclusive' (T022). There is no task to generate the third data point (Peak/Falling) or the logic to validate the full curve shape required by SC-001.

FR-005 mandates a report comparing accuracy for 'at least two distinct model backbones' to validate the regime map. While T021 generates a comparison, T022 explicitly marks the 'Collapse Regime' as 'Inconclusive'. There is no task to generate the specific 'Regime Map' visualization or report section that addresses the *failure* to validate the full curve, which is required to satisfy the FR's intent of validating the map.

US-02 acceptance scenario 2 requires recording an accuracy drop for a 'saturated (very large) model'. The plan and tasks (T018-T022) only implement TinyLlama and Phi-2. There is no task to execute or simulate a 'saturated' model run to satisfy this acceptance scenario.

T022 states: 'Add logic to explicitly mark "Collapse Regime" as "Inconclusive"'. This task references a regime (Collapse) that the implementation cannot test. The task description should be refined to 'Add logic to document the inability to validate the Collapse Regime due to compute constraints' to align with the plan's scope boundary.
